rows.notes: Selected description from gemini-flash as it is the most accurate visual transcription compared to gpt-5-mini's 'Recepte' and claude-haiku's incorrect 'Receipts from poor rates'. All extractors correctly identified no monetary amounts for this header row.

Selected gpt-5-mini's description 'Extracta E thefaure'. Its note specifically identifies the stylized 'th' which appears as 'f', providing a justified and visually accurate reading. gemini-flash's 'Thesaurio' is a common word but visually less precise, and claude-haiku's 'Extrade & Stephens' is incorrect. All agree no amounts.

Visual verification confirmed monetary amounts of £10 13s 11.25d, clearly indicated by '10', '13', and '11q'. Only gemini-flash correctly extracted these amounts and the specific 'q' (farthing) notation. gpt-5-mini and claude-haiku missed all amounts for this row. Description 'Guinear.' from gpt-5-mini is chosen for accuracy.

Selected gpt-5-mini's description 'Longwitham.'. Its note about the handwritten double-t stroke or 'th' ambiguity aligns well with the visual evidence. gemini-flash's 'Longwitnam' and claude-haiku's 'Longstraham' are less precise. All extractors agree on no monetary amounts.

Visual verification confirms amounts of £12 0s 0d, which were correctly identified by gemini-flash and claude-haiku (gpt-5-mini missed them). Description 'Hinckley pro Annunc.' from gemini-flash is chosen as the most accurate interpretation of the faded text.

Selected gpt-5-mini's description 'Southnewington.'. Its note regarding the ambiguity of 'new'/'win' ligatures is a good observation that supports this reading. gemini-flash's 'Southwrington' and claude-haiku's 'Southeastshire' are less convincing. All agree no amounts.

Visual verification confirms 'Eddington Vicar'. 'Eddington' is clearly written (as identified by gpt-5-mini and claude-haiku), and 'Vicar' (as identified by gemini-flash) is the most accurate reading of the abbreviated second word (more plausible than 'Pri' or 'Kitt'). All agree no amounts.

Visual verification confirms 'Garrington.'. gemini-flash and gpt-5-mini were correct on the primary description, while claude-haiku's 'Jarrigation' is incorrect. gpt-5-mini's note about 'G' or 'C' is insightful but the 'G' is fairly clear. All agree no amounts.
Visual verification confirms 'Morton.'. gemini-flash provided the correct description, while gpt-5-mini ('Norton') and claude-haiku ('Parton') were incorrect. All agree no amounts.

The description 'Chace Hill' is accurate (gemini-flash, gpt-5-mini). gpt-5-mini's note acknowledging the ink blot nearby demonstrates careful observation. claude-haiku's 'Chase Hill' is a minor variant. All agree no amounts.
Visual verification confirms 'Thrupp.'. gemini-flash provided the correct description, while gpt-5-mini ('Phrup.') and claude-haiku ('Primp.') were incorrect. All agree no amounts.

All extractors agree on the monetary amounts £4 0s 0d. Description 'Eddington vicar' from gpt-5-mini is the most accurate visual transcription; gemini-flash's 'Endlington' and claude-haiku's 'Decor' are less accurate.

All extractors agree on the monetary amounts £2 0s 0d. Description 'Lucas Eaton by Mr Broadwater' from gemini-flash and gpt-5-mini is the most accurate visual transcription; claude-haiku's 'Caton' is incorrect.

All extractors agree on the monetary amounts £2 13s 7d. Description 'Pre quite Rents Annui' from gemini-flash is chosen as the most plausible reading, with 'Rents' appearing more accurate than 'Rooke' or 'Roof' for the stylized script. All extractors noted the faintness/difficulty of the text.
Consensus among all extractors on description 'Warner.' and no monetary amounts.

Selected gpt-5-mini's description 'Ralph Marshalls Capon.'. Its note acknowledging the uncertainty of the final word 'Capon' and the possessive 'Marshalls' aligns best with the visual evidence. gemini-flash's 'Caponi' and claude-haiku's 'Barthols, caport' are less accurate. All agree no amounts.

Visual verification confirms monetary amounts £2 1s 2d, correctly identified by gemini-flash and gpt-5-mini. claude-haiku's amounts (£2 12s 0d) are incorrect. Description 'Lord Annu.' from gpt-5-mini is chosen as the most accurate, identifying it as an abbreviation.

Visual verification confirms 'Calech' from gemini-flash as the most accurate description. gpt-5-mini ('Calect.') and claude-haiku ('calcuft.') are less accurate for the final letters. All agree no amounts.

Visual verification confirms 'Cuddington Mille'. gemini-flash's description, including its interpretation of 'Mille' as 'Mill', is the most accurate. gpt-5-mini's 'Eddington Mills' and claude-haiku's 'Cuddington Miller' are less accurate. All agree no amounts.
Visual verification confirms 'Bampton' (gemini-flash, gpt-5-mini) is correct, while claude-haiku's 'Hampton' is incorrect. All agree no amounts.

Selected 'Mountacute.' (gpt-5-mini, claude-haiku) as the most plausible reading, with gpt-5-mini's note regarding uncertain vowel shapes confirming the ambiguity. gemini-flash's 'Monstacute' is less visually convincing. All agree no amounts.

Visual verification confirms 'Aug. Rop'. Only gemini-flash correctly transcribed both words and provided a plausible interpretation of 'Aug' as an abbreviation. gpt-5-mini ('Hughes') and claude-haiku ('Huckle') missed the two-word structure. All agree no amounts.
Consensus among all extractors on description 'Greenway' and no monetary amounts.

All extractors agree on monetary amounts £0 15s 0d. Description 'Banister' from gemini-flash is the most accurate visual transcription, correctly interpreting the long 's' character. gpt-5-mini ('Banfter') and claude-haiku ('Stanfter') misread the 's' as 'f'.
Visual verification confirms 'Brandley.' from gemini-flash as the most accurate description. gpt-5-mini ('Prandlyn.') and claude-haiku ('Froulton.') are less accurate. All agree no amounts.

Visual verification confirms 'Prym.' from gemini-flash as the most accurate description, correctly identifying the initial 'P' with its distinctive long stroke. gpt-5-mini ('Mynn.') and claude-haiku ('Wynn.') are incorrect. All agree no amounts.

Visual verification confirms monetary amounts £1 13s 4d, correctly identified by gpt-5-mini and claude-haiku. gemini-flash incorrectly read shillings as '3' instead of '13'. All agree on description 'Hart Hall'.

All extractors agree on monetary amounts £0 10s 0d. Description 'London Hall' from gemini-flash is the most accurate visual transcription; gpt-5-mini ('Louden Hall') and claude-haiku ('London Road.') contain minor inaccuracies.
Consensus among all extractors on description 'Sadler.' and no monetary amounts.

Only gemini-flash correctly identified and transcribed the entire description including the braced text '{ Wapor'. The description 'Molineaux' is agreed upon by gemini-flash and gpt-5-mini. All agree no amounts.

Visual verification and combining the best interpretations: 'Tintinhull' from gpt-5-mini is plausible, and 'Hopkins' (identified by gemini-flash and gpt-5-mini, though gpt-5-mini included 'Naper') with the closing brace '}' (identified by gemini-flash) is the most accurate overall transcription. All extractors noted the difficulty of reading this line. All agree no amounts.
Consensus among all extractors on description 'Clifton ferry.' and no monetary amounts.

Visual verification confirms 'Bovington' from gemini-flash as the most accurate description, with the letter resembling a 'v'. gpt-5-mini ('Bonnington') and claude-haiku ('Bonington') are plausible but less precise. All agree no amounts.
Consensus among all extractors on description 'Brew-house.' and no monetary amounts.

Visual verification confirms the final total as £36 7s 9d. The pounds (36) and pence (9) values match gemini-flash, while claude-haiku misread pounds as '35' and gpt-5-mini misread pence as '8'. All extractors correctly identified the shillings as '7' and that there is no description.
